QF.id: XC Investment Products and Market Developments_2

question.text: Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) differ from regular mutual fund schemes primarily because:

answer.text: ETFs invest only in government securities while mutual funds invest in equities
ETFs are listed and traded on stock exchanges like shares, offering real-time pricing during market hours
ETFs are not regulated by SEBI unlike regular mutual funds
ETFs guarantee capital protection while mutual funds do not

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The key differentiator of ETFs is that they are listed and traded on stock exchanges, providing real-time pricing and the ability to buy/sell at market prices during trading hours, unlike regular MF units priced at end-of-day NAV. **The Trap:** Both ETFs and MFs are regulated by SEBI. Neither guarantees capital protection. ETFs can invest in equities, bonds, gold, etc. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V-A covers ETFs under MF products. Series X-A discusses them as investment options. Series XII covers exchange-traded products. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ETF liquidity depends on the underlying market maker — check the bid-ask spread before recommending any ETF, as illiquid ETFs may trade at significant premiums or discounts to NAV.
